You can use optional chaining and nullish coalescing inside function call arguments to provide safe defaults without conditional blocks.
TypeScript tracks the possible types after each operator, so you can continue narrowing in subsequent branches without redundant checks.
Angular templates support the safe navigation operator (
) for exactly the same null-safety purpose as JavaScript's optional chaining. It prevents the template from throwing when an async value hasn't arrived yet.

{{ user?.name }}

{{ user?.address?.city }}

{{ user?.profile?.avatarUrl ?? '/assets/default-avatar.png' }}

@if (user$ | async; as user) {

{{ user.name }}

{{ user.address?.city ?? 'No city' }}

Signals in Angular 17+ hold values directly — they never return
for the signal itself, but the value they hold may be
. Optional chaining and nullish coalescing apply to the unwrapped value.

Optional chaining produces a value of the inner type or undefined. Type guards let you recover full type information after a chain.
Logical OR Assignment (||=) and AND Assignment (&&=)
for falsy-based and truthy-based initialization respectively.
When you are certain a value is not null or undefined but TypeScript's flow analysis cannot prove it, the non-null assertion operator (
) suppresses the null error. It is a compile-time-only assertion — no runtime check is added.

for cases where Angular's lifecycle guarantees the value — ViewChild/ContentChild after AfterViewInit, required @Input on a well-typed component. Do not use it to silence errors on optional values — use
Performance: Why ?. and ?? Are Not a Concern

Optional chaining and nullish coalescing are native JavaScript operators since ES2020 and are output as-is by the Angular compiler targeting modern browsers. There is no runtime overhead compared to manual null checks — the two forms compile to identical bytecode in V8 and other engines.
Modern Angular CLI projects target ES2022 or later by default. Check your
to confirm. If you need to support IE11 (extremely rare in new projects), the transpiled form above is generated automatically.

In Angular templates, use the safe navigation operator ?. for the same purpose; combine with the async pipe's as binding to eliminate null checks in the template body
